summary.what: Surface infrastructure-skipped commit count in timbers status
summary.why: Skip relaxations need a feedback channel — without visibility users can't tell when their .timbersignore is over-skipping; cheap one-line surfacing in status is the minimum that catches drift without building anti-abuse machinery.
summary.how: Added Storage.CountInfraSkippedSinceLatest (in skipcount.go) that walks (latestAnchor..HEAD), filters via the same skipRule set used for pending, and returns the count; status --verbose adds a key/value line and JSON always includes infra_skipped_since_entry.

notes: Reviewer wanted --verbose-only for human display to avoid surprising latency; complied. JSON always emits the field for machine consumers per snake_case convention. Errors and stale-anchor cases collapse to 0 silently — status is visibility, not enforcement, and a noisy error here would inverse the goal. Split out skipcount.go to keep storage.go under the 350-line limit and the count helper under the cyclop=10 ceiling.
